ip_outcome_note: Card depicts two figures: a light-haired youth and a blue-skinned humanoid. Vision identifies them as Trunks and Baby in Tuffle Parasite form. The Colossal Warfare set centers on the Baby Saga; Baby is canonically depicted as a lean blue-skinned parasite in humanoid form before fully possessing his hosts. Trunks (GT) appears as a Battle Card with Baby Saga era designation in this same set. The confrontation dynamic — young Saiyan fighter vs. blue parasite — matches Baby's early GT storyline where he initially targets Trunks.

trivia_bullets.text: In Dragon Ball GT's Baby Saga, Baby first targets Trunks as one of his early major host victims before ultimately possessing Vegeta. The confrontation depicted on this card — youth fighter vs. blue parasite form — reflects the early arc phase before Baby achieves his most powerful Vegeta possession. [Source: Dragon Ball Villains Wiki: Baby]

Baby's blue-skinned Tuffle Parasite form is his natural body — small, lean, and blue — before he enters and amplifies his host's physique. The design contrast between Baby's compact alien form and Trunks's humanoid Saiyan build on a duo-confrontation card efficiently codes the parasite-vs-host dynamic in a single frame. [Source: Dragon Ball Villains Wiki: Baby]

Colossal Warfare (DBS-B04, July 2018) designates Trunks: GT cards with Baby Saga era labels, making Trunks a supporting card type in Baby-themed deck strategies — the card game mechanically encodes the GT storyline's Trunks-as-early-target narrative into deck-building dynamics. [Source: Bleeding Cool: Colossal Warfare Checklist]
